ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for sliced duck in spring onion pastry specifically; closest_parent 'duck food product' (FOODON_03301116) used as the nearest defensible anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for duck pastry rolls; A0FKP (poultry-based composite dish) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept exists for this specific prepared duck pastry dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Sliced Duck in Crispy Spring Onion Pastry at Din Tai Fung consists of approximately 3 to 4 rolls weighing around 120 g in total.

nutrient_source.title: Duck, domesticated, meat only, cooked, roasted — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy; Spring roll wrapper (wheat-based, fried) — USDA FoodData Central; composite estimation for smoked duck in fried pastry
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for 'sliced duck in crispy spring onion pastry' exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for cooked duck meat (FDC ID 05140) and fried spring roll wrapper (FDC ID 168918), weighted approximately 60% duck meat and 40% fried pastry by mass, with minor adjustments for spring onion and seasoning contributions. Energy and macros cross-checked against analogous dim sum duck roll entries in peer-reviewed Southeast Asian food composition literature.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of wheat-based spring roll wrappers and analogous fried pastry dim sum items — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS et al. (2021) International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021, Am J Clin Nutr
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for sliced duck in crispy spring onion pastry. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on the dominant glycemic driver being the fried wheat-based spring roll pastry skin, which is analogous to fried spring rolls and wheat-based dim sum wrappers with GI values in the 50–60 range per the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021). Duck meat and spring onion contribute negligible glycemic load. Glycemic load per 120 g serving calculated as (55 × 16.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 1.20) / 100 = 10.9 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content approximately 420 mg per 100 g; a 120 g serving contributes approximately 504 mg sodium, representing about 22% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one or two rolls and avoid additional high-sodium condiments such as soy sauce or chilli dipping sauce when consuming this dish.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (estimated 55) driven by the fried wheat-based pastry wrapper; a standard serving delivers approximately 19.8 g of carbohydrate and a glycemic load of approximately 10.9.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should account for the moderate glycemic load of this dish within their total carbohydrate budget for the meal and pair it with non-starchy vegetables to blunt the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Combined saturated fat from duck skin and deep-fried pastry (approximately 3.9 g per 100 g) and moderate cholesterol content (approximately 72 mg per 100 g) are relevant cardiovascular risk factors.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat this dish as an occasional item, limiting intake to one serving and choosing steamed dim sum alternatives where possible to reduce saturated fat and cholesterol exposure.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sliced Duck in Crispy Spring Onion Pastry (葱香鸭卷, Cōng xiāng yā juǎn) is a signature dim sum dish popularised by Din Tai Fung, the internationally acclaimed Taiwanese restaurant chain with a prominent presence in Singapore. The dish consists of thin slivers of smoked or roasted duck breast layered with fragrant spring onions and tightly rolled inside a crispy, golden spring roll pastry skin that is deep-fried to a light, shattering crunch. The contrast between the tender, savoury duck meat and the delicate crispness of the pastry shell is the defining textural characteristic of the dish. Flavour notes include the mild smokiness of the duck, the fresh pungency of spring onion, and a subtle richness from the rendered duck fat. Nutritionally, the dish provides a moderate energy density of approximately 248 kcal per 100 g, with meaningful protein from the duck meat (approximately 16 g per 100 g) and a moderate fat content (approximately 13.8 g per 100 g) contributed by both the duck and the fried pastry. Sodium is moderately elevated at approximately 420 mg per 100 g due to seasoning and the smoked preparation of the duck. The glycemic index is estimated at 55 (low), driven primarily by the wheat-based fried pastry wrapper. Clinically, the dish is of moderate concern for individuals managing hypertension due to its sodium content, for those with Type 2 Diabetes due to its low glycemic load, and for those with cardiovascular disease due to its saturated fat and cholesterol content. It is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those following a halal diet without specific certification. The dish is a popular starter or sharing plate in Chinese dim sum settings and is commonly served at celebratory meals and family gatherings in Singapore.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for this specific dish was found in HPB Singapore Foodo or any primary regional food composition database. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for cooked duck meat and fried spring roll wrapper, weighted by approximate ingredient proportions. GI is an analogous estimate based on the wheat-based fried pastry component. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reliably reported for this composite dish in the source databases used. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entries used for this estimation. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the source data for this composite dish. All other -1 values reflect nutrients not reported in the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries used as the basis for this estimation. The dish is served at Din Tai Fung Singapore (Marina Bay Link Mall) and is listed on field survey data at SGD 12.21. Halal status is not confirmed for Din Tai Fung Singapore; is_halal is set to false pending certification verification. The trans_fat_g value of 0.1 g per 100 g reflects a small residual amount from the deep-frying process using commercial oils; this is not confirmed zero. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on analogous fried wheat-based dim sum pastry items from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021); no direct GI measurement for this specific dish was found.

Corrected a character mismatch in the Hindi alias within aliases_by_language to ensure perfect consistency with the flat aliases list. Verified all ontology match_type values are within allowed set (exact, closest_parent, none). Confirmed no zero core macros.
